```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parametros
lambda_hat <- sample(c(2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8), 1)
n          <- sample(c(50, 100, 200), 1)

## Respostas: margem de erro IC 95% para Poisson (grande amostra)
correct <- round(1.96 * sqrt(lambda_hat / n), 4)
w1 <- round(1.96 * lambda_hat / sqrt(n), 4)
w2 <- round(1.96 * sqrt(lambda_hat * n), 4)
w3 <- round(1.96 * lambda_hat / n, 4)
w4 <- round(1.96 * sqrt(lambda_hat) / n, 4)

all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
Para $X_i \overset{\text{iid}}{\sim} \text{Poisson}(\lambda)$, pelo TCL temos $\bar{X} \approx N(\lambda,\, \lambda/n)$ para $n$ grande. Com $\hat{\lambda} = \bar{X} = `r lambda_hat`$ e $n = `r n`$, a margem de erro do IC 95% e $1{,}96\sqrt{\hat{\lambda}/n}$. Esse valor e:

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
A margem de erro e $1{,}96\sqrt{\hat{\lambda}/n} = 1{,}96\sqrt{`r lambda_hat`/`r n`} = `r correct`$.

Erros comuns: usar $\hat{\lambda}/\sqrt{n}$ sem a raiz exterior; multiplicar $\hat{\lambda}$ por $n$; dividir $\hat{\lambda}$ por $n$ sem raiz; usar $\sqrt{\hat{\lambda}}/n$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap5S1Q10_IC_Poisson_TCL
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
